questions.question: What is the most effective notification management strategy?

options.text: Keep all notifications on so you don't miss anything important
Disable all non-essential notifications and batch-check remaining ones at scheduled times
Turn notifications off only during work hours
Use vibrate mode instead of sound

questions.explanation: The strongest evidence supports aggressive notification pruning: disable all non-essential alerts and check remaining ones at scheduled intervals (e.g., 3-4 times/day). This reduces attention fragmentation while maintaining access to important information.

questions.question: Which types of notifications should you consider keeping enabled?

options.text: All social media likes, comments, and follows
Only critical, time-sensitive communications: calls from key contacts, urgent work messages, emergency alerts
All email notifications to stay on top of your inbox

questions.explanation: Keep only truly critical, time-sensitive notifications: calls from key people, urgent work messages (if required by your job), and emergency alerts. Most notifications (email, social media, news) can be batched.

questions.question: What is 'batch checking' and why is it effective?

options.text: Checking all apps simultaneously while multitasking
Checking messages/emails at scheduled times (e.g., 9am, 1pm, 5pm) instead of responding to each notification immediately
Grouping similar apps together on your home screen

questions.explanation: Batch checking means checking messages, emails, and social media at designated times (e.g., 3-4 times/day) rather than continuously. This preserves focus blocks while ensuring you stay responsive.

questions.question: What does research show about the impact of disabling notifications?

options.text: People miss critical information and become less productive
Most people report improved focus, reduced stress, and no significant loss of important information
It only helps people with attention disorders
It has no measurable effect on wellbeing or productivity

questions.explanation: Studies show that disabling notifications improves focus, reduces stress and anxiety, and rarely leads to missing truly important information — most notifications are low-priority.

questions.question: Review your current notification settings. Which apps currently send you notifications? Which of these could you disable without negative consequences? Pick 3-5 to turn off this week.
